description: Overview entries to explain the SCOR namespace inside MISP.
name: SCOR - About

values.description: SCOR (Space, Cybersecurity, Operations, and Resilience) is a taxonomic and ontological namespace that classifies converged-space-systems intelligence along five orthogonal layers: environment (PCE), segment (SEG), service plane (SVC), asset class (AST), and analytic dimension (AN). A single cluster value carries concurrent tags across layers, so an incident is reachable from environment-, segment-, service-, asset-, or indicator-typed pivots in MISP and Maltego.

SCOR equips Space, Cybersecurity, Operations, and Resilience Platform Professionals with a five-layer classification model for confirmed and modeled indicators of compromise, indicators of attack, attack paths, threats, detection signatures, and resilience measures. The model covers environments outside IT-centric frameworks (aquatic, orbital, deep space), a ten-segment range from user to deep space (user, ground, launch, link, low altitude, high altitude, near space, aquatic, space, deep space), explicit control-plane / data-plane / hybrid service separation, and assets that include signals and firmware alongside software, hardware, data, and hybrid. UUIDs are stable across versions so tags persist through content evolution.

Technical capabilities that distinguish SCOR from IT-centric taxonomies such as ATT&CK Enterprise/ICS: multi-axis tagging on a single cluster value, environment and segment coverage for aerial, aquatic, orbital, and deep-space domains, service-plane separation for TT&C versus mission payload events, an explicit modeled-IoC class for low-frequency high-impact events, and asset categories that include RF/optical/acoustic signals and firmware. Pairs with the MISP and Maltego SCOR workbench for graph analysis over the five-layer tag schema.

capabilities: Multi-axis tag schema (PCE / SEG / SVC / AST / AN) attaches concurrently to a single cluster value, enabling N-dimensional pivots in MISP and Maltego

Environment and segment coverage for aquatic (subsea cables, undersea autonomy), low/high altitude (UAS, near-space platforms), orbital, and deep space - layers absent or shallow in ATT&CK Enterprise and ATT&CK ICS
Service-plane separation distinguishes telemetry, tracking, and command (control plane) from mission payload data (data plane) and bus-bridged events (hybrid)

Modeled IoC class alongside confirmed IoC supports hypothesis tracking for low-frequency high-impact events (ASAT debris cascades, coordinated subsea cuts, sustained GNSS denial) where observable telemetry is sparse
Asset taxonomy explicitly covers signals (RF, optical, acoustic) and firmware, supporting incidents whose primary artifacts are not file-based
Stable UUIDs and version semantics preserve tag identity across releases, so long-running graph queries remain valid as content evolves
Aligned to the MISP and Maltego SCOR workbench for tag-driven pivots across environment, segment, service, asset, and analytic dimensions

values.description: Worked examples of cross-domain incidents that SCOR classifies coherently where IT-only taxonomies cannot. Each example lists the cluster-value tag set across the five SCOR layers, illustrating how environment, segment, service, asset, and analytic dimensions combine on real events.

examples: UAS and counter-UAS engagements: Gatwick Airport closures during sustained drone incursions (December 2018); UAS and cruise-missile strikes on Abqaiq and Khurais Saudi Aramco facilities (September 2019); one-way attack-drone strike on Tower 22 (January 2024); persistent UAS incursions reported over US installations including Langley AFB (December 2023). SCOR tags: PCE=Aerial, SEG=Low Altitude, SVC=Control Plane, AST=Hardware+Signals, AN=IOC+IOA+Threat+Resilience

Subsea fibre disruption: Matsu Islands cable severance attributed to vessel anchor activity (February 2023); Red Sea damage to AAE-1, EIG, Seacom, and TGN cables during regional escalation (February 2024); Baltic Sea severance of BCS East-West and C-Lion1 coincident with Yi Peng 3 transit (November 2024); Estlink 2 power and communications cable incident linked to Eagle S (December 2024). SCOR tags: PCE=Aquatic, SEG=Link, SVC=Data Plane+Hybrid, AST=Hardware+Data, AN=IOC+IOA+Threat+Resilience

Space cyber and signals events: AcidRain wiper against Viasat KA-SAT modems on the morning of the 2022 invasion of Ukraine, with collateral loss of wind-turbine telemetry across Europe (February 2022); persistent GNSS spoofing and jamming observed in the Black Sea, Eastern Mediterranean, and Strait of Hormuz; Cosmos 1408 ASAT debris-generating event (November 2021). SCOR tags: PCE=Orbital+Terrestrial, SEG=Ground+Link+Space, SVC=Control Plane+Data Plane, AST=Software+Firmware+Signals, AN=IOC+IOA+Threat+Detection+Resilience

values.description: PCE (environment) - Terrestrial, Aerial, Aquatic, Orbital, Deep Space. SEG (segment) - User, Ground, Launch, Link, Low Altitude, High Altitude, Near Space, Aquatic, Space, Deep Space. SVC (service) - Control Plane, Data Plane, Hybrid. AST (asset) - Software, Firmware, Hardware, Data, Signals, Hybrid. AN (analytic) - IOC (confirmed and modeled), IOA, Attack Path, Threat, Detection, Resilience.
Propose changes via the SCOR repository. Keep UUIDs stable. Bump version when IDs or descriptions change.

meta.policy: UUIDs immutable - version bump on content change
